_comment: Gate V2 reference values, PROJECTED formulation (M6.3). Supersedes the 2026-07-05 unprojected benchmarks per ROADMAP M6.3's BLOCKS-ON clause. Authored and committed by the human maintainer; computed by the independent Python/NumPy+SciPy reference (exact direct solves). The Gate V2 test must be updated to this formulation by the M6.3 agent.

formulation_locked.chain: x (design) --density filter H, rmin--> rho_tilde --Heaviside projection--> rho_bar --clamp[rho_min,1]--> E = rho_bar_clamped^p * E0
formulation_locked.projection: rho_bar = (tanh(beta*eta) + tanh(beta*(rho_tilde-eta))) / (tanh(beta*eta) + tanh(beta*(1-eta))), eta = 0.5 (single-field; Wang/Lazarov/Sigmund 2011). Derivative: d rho_bar/d rho_tilde = beta * sech^2(beta*(rho_tilde-eta)) / (tanh(beta*eta) + tanh(beta*(1-eta))). Sensitivities chain the derivative BEFORE the filter transpose: dc/dx = H^T((dc/drho_bar * drho_bar/drho_tilde)/Hs); dv likewise.
formulation_locked.volume_constraint: on the PROJECTED density: mean(rho_bar) <= volfrac; OC bisection tests the projected volume of the candidate design.
formulation_locked.beta_schedule: beta in {1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32}, exactly 50 OC iterations per stage, 300 total. beta = 64 is EXCLUDED: measured on these benchmark meshes, the transition band (~1/beta) becomes narrower than the design oscillation and continuation destroys the structure (compliance explodes 4-5 orders of magnitude). The maintainer must amend ROADMAP M6.3's 'beta 1 -> 64' to 'beta 1 -> 32' to match.
formulation_locked.move_schedule: OC move limit 0.2 for beta <= 8, 0.1 at beta = 16, 0.05 at beta = 32. Undamped 0.2 at high beta produces structure-splitting oscillation (measured).
formulation_locked.oc_guard_locked: The elementwise OC ratio -dc/(lambda*dv) MUST be computed with UNCLAMPED dv. At high beta the projection derivative underflows toward 0 for voxels far from the threshold; dc and dv both carry that factor, so their RATIO stays well-conditioned. Where the ratio is non-finite (0/0: a voxel whose whole filter neighborhood has zero projection derivative), HOLD the design variable (Be = 1). Clamping dv at an absolute floor (e.g. 1e-12) silently breaks the ratio and drags exactly the should-not-move voxels to the bound at move-limit speed, destroying the design at beta >= 32 - this failure was measured during fixture generation (compliance 39.5 -> 2.2e6) and any implementation that clamps dv will diverge from these references catastrophically.
formulation_locked.filter: density filter as in the superseded fixture, but rmin = 2.5 (ARCHITECTURE section 4 requires only >= 1.5). Measured: rmin = 1.5 under eta = 0.5 projection forbids the 1-voxel members this coarse mesh otherwise uses (a lone strut's own filtered density peaks ~0.33 < eta), so continuation amputates the beta <= 16 design's sub-threshold connective tissue and the structure disintegrates at beta >= 32.
formulation_locked.everything_else: identical to the superseded fixture: material law E = clamp(rho,1e-3,1)^3 * E0, hex element per hex_stiffness_reference.json, OC eta 0.5 damping, bisection (l2-l1)/(l1+l2) < 1e-3 on [0, 1e9], initial x = volfrac uniform.
formulation_locked.solver_note: Reference used exact direct solves. C++ CG tolerance <= 1e-9 recommended; the smooth-regime gates below are solver-insensitive at that tolerance, the endpoint gates are bands.

projection_unit_reference._comment: Analytic spot values for unit-testing project() and its derivative exactly (rel tol 1e-12). eta = 0.5.

cantilever_24x8x8.problem: IDENTICAL geometry/BCs/loads/material/volfrac to the superseded fixture (clamp x=0 face; total Fz = -1 over the 81 tip-face nodes; E0=1, nu=0.3, volfrac=0.3).
cantilever_24x8x8.limit_cycle_note: At beta = 32 this problem settles into a stable period-2 cycle: the pre-update analysis samples ~91.8 while the post-update state analyzes to ~40.3, reproducibly (40.33 and 40.28 across independent 50- and 100-iteration stage runs). The endpoint is therefore gated as a BAND and the stage-32 sample is NOT gated. A solver-phase flip landing an implementation on the other phase would still fail the band - if that happens, extend the final stage by 50 iterations and re-evaluate before suspecting the physics.

clamped_beam_48x8x8.problem: IDENTICAL geometry/BCs/loads/material/volfrac to the superseded fixture (clamp both end faces; total Fz = -1 over the 9 top-center-line nodes).
clamped_beam_48x8x8.convergence_note: This problem converges to a true fixed point under the locked spec: stage-32 end (7.4801) and the final post-update analysis (7.4821) differ by 0.03%, discreteness Mnd = 0.015, fully monotone stage trajectory. It is the tight anchor of the pair.

honest_limitations: On these deliberately coarse benchmark meshes the 2x2x2 min-feature count does NOT go to zero under projection (thin plate-like members satisfy the filter-scale constraint while violating the voxel-cover metric; measured 368 cantilever / 288 beam). Projection's measurable win here is discreteness (Mnd 0.063 / 0.015 vs ~0.27 unprojected) and structural cleanliness; M5.2b's report warning remains the user-facing backstop for residual thin features, exactly as ROADMAP M6.3's task text anticipates. App-scale grids (48-128 resolution) have proportionally wider members and should fare better; that expectation is NOT gated here.
